title: SplitBodyFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing split body features in a component and supports the ability to create new split body features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new split body feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a SplitBodyFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the split body you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SplitBodyFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified split body feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified split body feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of split body features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Split Body Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split body feature.

add.title: SplitBodyFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new split body feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created SplitBodyFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A SplitBodyFeatureInput object that defines the desired split body feature. Use the createInput method to create a new SplitBodyFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the SplitBodyFeatureInput object) to define the split body.

related_samples.name: Split Body Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split body feature.

classType.title: SplitBodyFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: SplitBodyFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a SplitBodyFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the split body you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SplitBodyFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created SplitBodyFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: splitBodies
parameters.description: Input solid body or open bodies to be split. This can be a single BRepBody or an ObjectCollection if multiple bodies are to be split.

parameters.name: splittingTool
parameters.description: Input entity that defines the splitting tool. The splitting tool is a single entity that can be either a solid or open BRepBody, construction plane, profile, or a face.

parameters.name: isSplittingToolExtended
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not the splitting tool is to be automatically extended (if possible) so as to completely intersect the bodyToSplit.

related_samples.name: Split Body Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split body feature.

item.title: SplitBodyFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified split body feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: SplitBodyFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified split body feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: SplitBodyFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of split body features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitBodyFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: SplitBodyFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitBodyFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: SplitBodyFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitBodyFeatures_var.objectType
